changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for consistency with resource naming
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security features relevant to intrusion detection. Audit logging and retention are crucial for monitoring, and admission controllers can enforce security policies. Network policies can be used to detect anomalies, aligning with intrusion detection. Naming improvements were made for consistency with existing patterns.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource names like 'apiserver' are used for audit-related functions to maintain consistency with K8s terminology.
title: System Monitoring | System-wide Intrusion Detection System
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP SI-4 (1) control requires system-wide intrusion detection, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring comprehensive audit logging and retention policies are in place, as well as leveraging admission controllers for security policy enforcement. Additionally, network policies can be configured to detect and respond to anomalous traffic patterns, serving as a form of intrusion detection.
